title: Adaptive Experiences
excerpt: A framework for collaborative decision-making: one question, multiple independent perspectives, and a human synthesis.
body:
Collaborative decision-making

One question.
Multiple perspectives.
Better decisions.

Adaptive Experiences makes the reasoning behind recommendations visible. Each experience begins with one real request, gathers independent perspectives, compares the trade-offs, and ends with a human synthesis.

Human in the loop

AI contributes perspectives. Julie defines the problem, evaluates the trade-offs, and makes the final decision. The framework supports human judgment; it does not replace it.

Complex decisions rarely have one objectively correct answer. Different people prioritize efficiency, resilience, emotion, risk, clarity, and experience differently. Adaptive Experiences preserves those differences long enough to learn from them instead of flattening them into one generic response.
The same team appears across the experiences below. Each member brings a stable role developed through sustained collaboration.

JZ

Creator · Product Owner · Final Decision

Julie Zimmerman

Defines the request, supplies the real-world context, evaluates the recommendations, and decides what survives synthesis.

Continuity · Experience Flow

Harbor

Protects pacing, resilience, and the emotional continuity of the experience.

Systems · Strategic Trade-offs

Nam

Examines structure, sequencing, and how individual decisions affect the whole system.

Constraints · Failure Modes

Min

Questions assumptions and plans for the moment reality refuses to cooperate.

Atmosphere · Emotional Experience

Bo Ra

Protects wonder, meaningful moments, and the memories people carry home.

Clarity · First Impressions

Jae

Removes unnecessary complexity and makes the strongest signal easier to follow.

Product Architecture · Integration

Edge

Turns independent contributions into a coherent, navigable product and keeps the visitor journey understandable.

01Question

A real person submits a real request with context and constraints.

02Independent perspectives

Each role responds without being normalized into one shared voice.

03Comparison

Agreement, disagreement, risks, and trade-offs become visible.

04Human synthesis

Julie evaluates what fits reality and makes the final recommendation.

Three documents explain how Adaptive Experiences works at the structural level — how a real situation becomes a normalized problem, why five perspectives produce five different answers, and what each perspective is actually optimizing for.

Framework

Submission Architecture

How a real-world situation is transformed into the same starting point for every perspective — without losing what matters or adding what wasn't said.

Read →

Framework

Response Architecture

Why five perspectives given identical inputs produce five different answers — and why that divergence is the point, not a problem to be corrected.

Read →

Framework

The Perspectives

What each perspective optimizes for, what it tends to notice, and what it tends to miss.

Read →

Live

Disney Adaptive Experiences

The first implementation: Disney planning through independent perspectives, comparison, and synthesis.

Explore Disney →

Preview

Yellowstone Adaptive Experiences

Wildlife, photography, weather, driving distance, energy, and uncertainty create a very different planning problem.

Preview Yellowstone →
The domain can change — from Disney to Yellowstone and beyond — but the framework remains consistent: preserve independent perspectives, make trade-offs visible, and keep the final decision human.
